message:
refactor(robot-schema): extract per-section response-schema helpers

The inline schema definitions embedded in the robot-mode response
handlers had grown large and duplicative: every endpoint spelled out
its own index/state/rebuild/checkpoint property trees, so drift between
actual response shapes and declared schemas was common.

Extract the recurring sub-schemas into dedicated helper functions:
- response_schema_index_state (incl. fingerprint + checkpoint blocks,
  empty_with_messages flag)
- response_schema_state_database
- response_schema_status_database (reuses state_database and augments)
- response_schema_health_db
- response_schema_pending_state
- response_schema_rebuild_state
- response_schema_semantic_state
- response_schema_state_meta
- response_schema_index_freshness

Callers now compose these instead of inlining. Net: same schema
coverage with ~130 lines of duplication removed and a single place to
update when a field is added. This also closes the gap where index
freshness / checkpoint fields present in the runtime payload were
missing from the declared schema.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
